purpose: Validate identifiers before canonical records are linked. This registry does not rename source-level IDs.

namespaces.note: This namespace type did not previously exist in this registry, though the ch01-is-NNN ID pattern itself was already established and in use by CANONICAL_DESIGN_SCHEMA.json's own instructorScript record convention, across the four pilot topics (ch01-is-102/103/108/110) and Batch 1 (ch01-is-101/104). Added at registryVersion 1.3.0 to close this gap as part of Batch 1 identifier registration; does not retroactively register the four pilot topics' own instructorScript IDs.

This namespace type did not previously exist in this registry, though the ch01-tNN-block-<type> ID pattern itself was already established and in use by CANONICAL_DESIGN_SCHEMA.json's own contentBlock record convention, across the four pilot topics and Batch 1. Added at registryVersion 1.3.0 to close this gap as part of Batch 1 identifier registration; does not retroactively register the four pilot topics' own contentBlock IDs.

^ch01-t[0-9]{2}-visual-[0-9]{3}$ was added at registryVersion 1.3.0 to cover the produced visual-asset ID convention already in use by the four pilot topics (ch01-t02-visual-001, ch01-t03-visual-001, ch01-t08-visual-001, ch01-t10-visual-001) and by Batch 1 (ch01-t01-visual-001, ch01-t04-visual-001), which had no matching pattern in this namespace before 1.3.0. This is distinct from ^ch01-vis-[0-9]{3}$, which names visualResolutionId (audit/planning-tracking) records in VISUAL_RESOLUTION_PLAN.json, not produced-asset IDs.

D[0-9]{2} covers duplicateGroups (D01-D05); C[0-9]{2} covers the separate canonicalConflicts series (C01-C04) in the same source file. Both are audit-layer IDs from docs/content-audits/chapter-01/duplicates-and-conflicts.json and are distinguished by prefix letter only.

validationRules: An ID must match exactly one compatible namespace pattern.
A matching pattern is insufficient: the ID must also exist in the namespace authority file unless it is a new canonical design ID.
Locator type and locator namespace must agree.
Inferred cross-source links retain their original IDs and declare linkageType=inferred.
Unknown or ambiguous identifiers set blockingReason to conflictingSource or missingSource.
No source-level identifier is rewritten to fit a canonical namespace.

conflictNamespaceFix.issue: C01-C04 (canonicalConflicts in docs/content-audits/chapter-01/duplicates-and-conflicts.json) matched no registered conflict pattern prior to registryVersion 1.1.0.
conflictNamespaceFix.resolution: Added ^C[0-9]{2}$ as a distinct pattern in the conflict namespace.
conflictNamespaceFix.collisionCheck: Checked ^C[0-9]{2}$ against all other registered patterns in every namespace; no existing ID (SEG*, S2-SEG*, S3-P*, R1-P*, R1-CARD*, S5-P*, ch01-prob-*, S5-OCC*, ch01-sol-*, R1-Q*, S3C-Q*, ch01-quiz-*, V*, R1-VIS-*, S5-VIS-MISSING-*, ch01-vis-*, SCA*, S2-SCI-*, S3-SCI-*, S3C-SCI-*, S5-SCI-*, R1-SCI-*, D[0-9]{2}, S2-DUP-*, S5-DUP-*, S5-CONF-*, CD-CONF-*, ch01-corr-*, ch01-term-*) matches ^C[0-9]{2}$, and C01-C04 match no other pattern. Each real ID now matches exactly one namespace pattern.

batch1NamespaceAdditions.issue: Prior to registryVersion 1.3.0, this registry had no namespace type for instructorScript or contentBlock record IDs at all (despite both being established, in-use CANONICAL_DESIGN_SCHEMA.json record types across the four pilot topics and Batch 1), and its "visual" namespace type had no pattern matching the produced visual-asset ID convention ch01-tNN-visual-NNN (as distinct from ch01-vis-NNN, which names a visualResolutionId planning/audit record, not a produced asset).
batch1NamespaceAdditions.resolution: Added two new namespace types (instructorScript: ^ch01-is-[0-9]{3}$; contentBlock: ^ch01-t[0-9]{2}-block-[a-z]+$) and one new pattern within the existing visual namespace type (^ch01-t[0-9]{2}-visual-[0-9]{3}$), each referencing CANONICAL_DESIGN_SCHEMA.json as authority file, mirroring the existing problem/solution namespace types' own use of that same authority file.
batch1NamespaceAdditions.collisionCheck: Checked all three new patterns against every ID actually present in the approved Batch 1 files (ch01-is-101, ch01-is-104, all twelve ch01-t01-block-*/ch01-t04-block-* blockIds, ch01-prob-104, SRC-CH01-BATCH1-ORIGINAL, ch01-t01-visual-001, ch01-t04-visual-001) and against every other registered namespace pattern. Result: ch01-prob-104 continues to match only the existing problem pattern; SRC-CH01-BATCH1-ORIGINAL continues to match only the existing source pattern; every instructorScript/contentBlock/visual-asset ID now matches exactly one namespace pattern (its own new one) and matched none previously. No existing registered pattern or ID collides with any of the three additions.
batch1NamespaceAdditions.scopeNote: These are schema/namespace-validation additions only. They do not themselves register any specific identifier — see batch1IdentifierRegistration below for the actual Batch 1 registration entries. The four pilot topics' own instructorScript/contentBlock/visual-asset IDs, which already conform to these same newly-added patterns, remain unregistered as individual entries; this task registers Batch 1 identifiers only.

batch1IdentifierRegistration.registeredBy: khubrani2a-gif (project owner), via Claude-executed controlled registration task
batch1IdentifierRegistration.scopeStatement: This registers exactly the Batch 1 content, problem, source-marker, and approved visual identifiers required before a future application-integration step. It does not integrate ch01-t01 or ch01-t04 into apps/chapter1-mvp/, does not modify any approved content or visual file, does not authorize student-facing use, and does not authorize publication. Registration is an internal governance bookkeeping action, not a content-approval or application action.

glossaryTermIdsExcluded.statement: Glossary term IDs are governed inside BILINGUAL_GLOSSARY.json (glossaryVersion 1.3.0) and are deliberately NOT registered as entries in this file, preserving the established decision that glossary identifiers are glossary-local.

entries.notes: Same instructorScriptId used, identically, in both the approved English and Arabic candidate files.

entries.registrationBasis: ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.6.0 revisionLog rev-003/rev-004/rev-005/rev-006 (rev-007/rev-008 do not change this record's own text, only its blockType/slideNumber/slideTitleEn tagging); ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.0.6 revisionLog rev-001/rev-002/rev-003/rev-004 (rev-005/rev-006 do not change this record's own text, only its blockType/slideNumber/slideTitleEn/slideTitleAr tagging)
entries.notes: Generic, reusable "slide" blockType (not one of the original seven ContentBlockType values), shared with ch01-t01-block-opening-2 and any future slide record — see src/types/pilotSchema.ts's ContentBlockType header note. Was originally registered under the per-slide value "openingConcept" (rev-006-era); ch01-english-baseline-rev-008 / ch01-arabic-baseline-rev-006 migrated it to the generic "slide" value plus slideNumber (1) and slideTitleEn/slideTitleAr metadata, with no change to blockId or instructional text. Unlike this topic's other six original contentBlock identifiers, this one was not covered by a dedicated separate PHSH111_BATCH1_APPLICATION_INTEGRATION_AUTHORIZATION_RECORD.md-style document at registration time; its rendering in apps/chapter1-mvp/ is authorized directly by the project owner's request in the implementation task that added it, consistent with applicationIntegrationState remaining 'unauthorized' here in the same historical-snapshot sense as every other entry in this registry.

entries.registrationBasis: ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.6.0 revisionLog rev-007/rev-008; ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.0.6 revisionLog rev-005/rev-006
entries.notes: Generic, reusable "slide" blockType (shared with ch01-t01-block-opening; was originally registered under the per-slide value "openingConceptSlide2", migrated by ch01-english-baseline-rev-008 / ch01-arabic-baseline-rev-006 to the generic value plus slideNumber (2) and slideTitleEn/slideTitleAr metadata — see src/types/pilotSchema.ts's ContentBlockType header note and src/content/adapter.ts's normalizeSlides). Its provenance source is recorded inline on the record itself via provenanceLinks[0].sourceId = "SRC-OWNER-SLIDE-1.1-DISTANCE" (an uploaded project-owner slide reference, following the same free-form ad hoc source-marker convention already used by ch01-t01-block-opening's "SRC-OWNER-SLIDE-1.1" — neither is registered under the ^SRC-CH01-[A-Z0-9-]+$ namespace pattern, since that pattern is reserved for the chapter's independently audited sources, not project-owner-supplied slide references). A requested Figure Explanation subsection (an uploaded slide figure of racing vehicles using parachutes) was intentionally omitted by explicit project-owner decision — the figure is illustrative and not required for understanding this slide's concept, not an unresolved gap. blocking.blockingReason no longer includes 'missingVisual' (removed by ch01-english-baseline-rev-008 / ch01-arabic-baseline-rev-006) and visualBaselineDependency remains null; no provisional visual record was created. Its rendering in apps/chapter1-mvp/ is authorized directly by the project owner's request in the implementation task that added it, consistent with applicationIntegrationState remaining 'unauthorized' here in the same historical-snapshot sense as every other entry in this registry.

entries.registrationBasis: ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.0.0; ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.0.0; VISUAL_BATCH1_APPROVAL.json v1.0.0
entries.notes: This contentBlock's own visualGovernance.availabilityStatus field inside the approved topic JSON was NOT modified by this registration; it still reads "missing" and is not updated by this task, per instruction not to alter the topic JSON files.
visibility: instructor in the approved topic JSON; unaffected by this registration.

Same instructorScriptId used, identically, in both the approved English and Arabic candidate files.

entries.notes: This contentBlock's own visualGovernance.availabilityStatus field inside the approved topic JSON was NOT modified by this registration; it still reads "missing" and is not updated by this task, per instruction not to alter the topic JSON files.
visibility: instructor in the approved topic JSON; unaffected by this registration.
Registered as the canonical schema blockType reviewQuestion. "Review Card" is an application-facing UI label only and is not used as the registered identifierType, recordType, or blockType here.
sourceVariants[0].sourceId references SRC-CH01-BATCH1-ORIGINAL (see that entry below); this problem is fully original, not derived from any audited source's scenario, wording, object, or numbers.

entries.registrationBasis: ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.0.0; used as sourceVariants[0].sourceId on ch01-prob-104
entries.notes: Registered strictly as a project-authorship/source-lineage marker. It represents newly authored Batch 1 project content. It is NOT an external audited source, NOT a textbook/slide/review-card/publisher source, and must NOT be treated as independent evidence for any scientific claim. It is distinct from an audited source record (e.g. SRC-CH01-CONV-001, SRC-CH01-PDF-003), a scientific-audit record (e.g. SCA01, S3-SCI-004), and a correction record (e.g. ch01-corr-001, ch01-corr-002) — those substantive scientific records retain their own audited source and correction lineage, entirely independent of this marker.
Independent human visual review incomplete (reviewer/reviewedAt remain null on the validation record). Neither the SVG nor its validation record was modified by this registration.
Independent human visual review incomplete (reviewer/reviewedAt remain null on the validation record). Neither the SVG nor its validation record was modified by this registration.

entries.registrationBasis: ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.8.0 revisionLog rev-010; ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.0.8 revisionLog rev-008
entries.notes: Generic, reusable "slide" blockType (shared with ch01-t01-block-opening and ch01-t01-block-opening-2 — see src/types/pilotSchema.ts's ContentBlockType header note and src/content/adapter.ts's normalizeSlides); slideNumber 3, added purely as data with no new ContentBlockType, NormalizedTopic field, adapter branch, or TopicPage conditional, demonstrating the reusable-slide architecture established by rev-008/ch01-arabic-baseline-rev-006. Also introduces generic tableEn/tableAr fields (see src/types/pilotSchema.ts's SourceTable), a mechanism available to any content-block record, used here to reconstruct the project owner's uploaded source table (Physical Quantity / Metric Units / English Units) as a real semantic <table> rather than the screenshot itself. Its provenance source is recorded inline on the record itself via provenanceLinks[0].sourceId = "SRC-OWNER-SLIDE-1.1-DISTANCE-UNITS-TABLE" (an uploaded project-owner slide image, following the same free-form ad hoc source-marker convention already used by ch01-t01-block-opening / ch01-t01-block-opening-2). blocking.blockingReason does not include 'missingVisual': the uploaded source table was fully available and used, so no visual asset remains missing. Its rendering in apps/chapter1-mvp/ is authorized directly by the project owner's request in the implementation task that added it, consistent with applicationIntegrationState remaining 'unauthorized' here in the same historical-snapshot sense as every other entry in this registry. Updated by rev-010/rev-008: pedagogical wording review clarified the Simple Example's unit-consistency sentence, relabeled the dimensions calculation block (now 'Dimensions:' / 'الأبعاد:' with an added no-volume-calculation clarification sentence, avoiding any implied volume computation), and refined the Scientific Note's decimal-multiple/submultiple wording — no change to the original quotation, the source table, the steps, the Table Explanation, the Common Misconception, or the Key Concept.

entries.registrationBasis: ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.9.0 revisionLog rev-011; ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.0.10 revisionLog rev-009/rev-010
entries.notes: Generic, reusable "slide" blockType (shared with ch01-t01-block-opening, ch01-t01-block-opening-2, and ch01-t01-block-opening-3 — see src/types/pilotSchema.ts's ContentBlockType header note and src/content/adapter.ts's normalizeSlides); slideNumber 4, added purely as data with no new ContentBlockType, NormalizedTopic field, adapter branch, or TopicPage conditional. Also introduces generic figureAssetPath/figureAltEn/figureAltAr fields (see src/types/pilotSchema.ts's header note) — a mechanism available to any content-block record, used here to embed the project owner's uploaded coin photograph directly as this slide's figure (no cropping required; the upload was already a clean, tightly-framed image of only the coin and its two measurement labels). visualBaselineDependency remains null (this is a slide-embedded figure, not the topic-singular SVG visual tracked by visualGovernance/VISUAL_BATCH1_APPROVAL.json). Its provenance source is recorded inline on the record itself via provenanceLinks[0].sourceId = "SRC-OWNER-SLIDE-1.1-DIFFERENT-UNITS" (an uploaded project-owner slide image, following the same free-form ad hoc source-marker convention already used by Slides 1-3). blocking.blockingReason does not include 'missingVisual': the uploaded figure was fully available and used directly. Its rendering in apps/chapter1-mvp/ is authorized directly by the project owner's request in the implementation task that added it, consistent with applicationIntegrationState remaining 'unauthorized' here in the same historical-snapshot sense as every other entry in this registry. Updated by rev-010: project-owner Arabic language review refined slideTitleAr and figureAltAr wording (no factual/scientific change; English text unchanged).

entries.registrationBasis: ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.10.0 revisionLog rev-012; ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.0.12 revisionLog rev-011/rev-012
entries.notes: Generic, reusable "slide" blockType (shared with Slides 1-4 — see src/types/pilotSchema.ts's ContentBlockType header note and src/content/adapter.ts's normalizeSlides); slideNumber 5, added purely as data with no new ContentBlockType, NormalizedTopic field, adapter branch, or TopicPage conditional. Reuses the generic figureAssetPath/figureAltEn/figureAltAr fields and RAW_FIGURE_URL_BY_BLOCK_ID mechanism introduced for Slide 4 (rev-011) — this time with a hand-authored SVG diagram rather than a raster photograph, demonstrating the mechanism is asset-format-agnostic. The SVG recreates only the rectangle (height 1 ft, width 1.96 ft) from the project-owner-referenced source slide; the source photo's coin fragment (bleeding in from the adjacent, already-integrated Slide 4) and the basketball are deliberately excluded, per explicit project-owner instruction, rather than retained as an unnecessary raster crop. visualBaselineDependency remains null (this is a slide-embedded figure, not the topic-singular SVG visual tracked by visualGovernance/VISUAL_BATCH1_APPROVAL.json). Its provenance source is recorded inline on the record itself via provenanceLinks[0].sourceId = "SRC-OWNER-SLIDE-1.1-AREA-VOLUME" (a project-owner-referenced slide image, following the same free-form ad hoc source-marker convention already used by Slides 1-4). blocking.blockingReason does not include 'missingVisual': the rectangle diagram was fully reconstructable from the source slide's own labeled dimensions. Its rendering in apps/chapter1-mvp/ is authorized directly by the project owner's request in the implementation task that added it, consistent with applicationIntegrationState remaining 'unauthorized' here in the same historical-snapshot sense as every other entry in this registry. Updated by rev-012: project-owner Arabic scientific-language review corrected three sentences (size-of-a-surface wording changed from 'حجم السطح' to 'مقدار امتداد السطح' to avoid confusion with the physical quantity Volume; Step 1's area/volume derivation bullets revised for grammatical consistency) — no factual/scientific claim changed, English text and figure unchanged.

entries.registrationBasis: ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.11.0 revisionLog rev-013; ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json v1.0.13 revisionLog rev-013
entries.notes: Generic, reusable "slide" blockType (shared with Slides 1-5 — see src/types/pilotSchema.ts's ContentBlockType header note and src/content/adapter.ts's normalizeSlides); slideNumber 6, added purely as data with no new ContentBlockType, NormalizedTopic field, adapter branch, TopicPage conditional, or per-slide accordion JSX (renders automatically as the accordion's sixth item through the existing generic topic.slides[] mapping in src/features/topics/Slides.tsx). Reuses the generic tableEn/tableAr source-table mechanism introduced for Slide 3 (rev-009/rev-010), extended in this revision with two new generic, blockType-independent fields (tableCaptionEn/tableCaptionAr — mirroring the slideTitleEn/slideTitleAr convention) so the reconstructed table carries a <caption>, plus a presentation-only, non-slide-specific enhancement to the shared table renderer giving non-null first-column cells (here 'Area (A)'/'Volume (V)') semantic <th scope="rowgroup"> grouping via rowSpan. The reconstructed table uses scientifically formatted superscripts (m², cm², m³, etc.); the verbatim Original English/Arabic quoted prose instead preserves the source's own plain notation (m2, cm2, m3, etc.) unaltered, per explicit project-owner instruction. The short accordion title ('Area and Volume Units' / 'وحدات المساحة والحجم') is resolved entirely at the application layer via src/content/slideShortTitles.ts's existing UI-only lookup keyed by this record's blockId — no field for it exists on, or was added to, this approved content record. visualBaselineDependency remains null (this is a slide-embedded reconstructed table, not the topic-singular SVG visual tracked by visualGovernance/VISUAL_BATCH1_APPROVAL.json). Its provenance source is recorded inline on the record itself via provenanceLinks[0].sourceId = "SRC-OWNER-SLIDE-1.1-AREA-VOLUME-UNITS-TABLE" (a project-owner-uploaded slide image at /mnt/data/9AD6874A-B410-4B74-8778-4F50F0B1E444.png, following the same free-form ad hoc source-marker convention already used by Slides 1-5). The uploaded screenshot itself is explicitly NOT used as the student-facing visual. blocking.blockingReason does not include 'missingVisual': the table was fully reconstructable from the source slide's own listed units and was used directly. Its rendering in apps/chapter1-mvp/ is authorized directly by the project owner's request in the implementation task that added it, consistent with applicationIntegrationState remaining 'unauthorized' here in the same historical-snapshot sense as every other entry in this registry.

unchangedByThisRegistration: No approved English or Arabic topic JSON file was modified; both remain byte-identical to their baseline-approval checksums.
Neither approved SVG nor either visual-validation record was modified; both remain byte-identical to VISUAL_BATCH1_APPROVAL.json's recorded checksums.
BILINGUAL_GLOSSARY.json remains unchanged, still glossaryVersion 1.3.0; no glossary term ID was registered here.
No route, import, or registry entry was added anywhere in apps/chapter1-mvp/.
applicationIntegrationAuthorized remains false for both topics.
studentFacingAllowed and studentPublicationAuthorized remain false everywhere.

registrationHistory.scope: Batch 1 identifier registration (ch01-t01, ch01-t04): registered the 18 identifiers required before application integration (2 instructorScript, 12 contentBlock, 1 problem, 1 source-authorship marker, 2 visual assets), and added the instructorScript and contentBlock namespace types plus the ch01-tNN-visual-NNN pattern within the existing visual namespace type, all previously missing from this registry despite being already-established CANONICAL_DESIGN_SCHEMA.json ID conventions in use by the four pilot topics and Batch 1 alike.
registrationHistory.applicationAndPublicationRestrictions: This registration does not authorize application integration, student-facing use, or publication for ch01-t01 or ch01-t04. applicationIntegrationAuthorized, studentFacingAllowed, and studentPublicationAuthorized all remain false.
registrationHistory.projectOwnerDecisionBasis: Project-owner explicit request to register the approved Batch 1 identifiers required before application integration, following the same controlled-registration pattern implied by the existing conflict-namespace precedent (registryVersion 1.1.0) and by each topic's own identifierRegistrationStatus.pending notes in ENGLISH_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json, ARABIC_BATCH1_BASELINE_APPROVAL.json, and VISUAL_BATCH1_APPROVAL.json.
